title: Imposter
style: vocal-forward, spacious, ambient folk, vulnerable and raw, reverbed acoustic guitar plus ambient pads and sparse piano and breath sounds, male vocal raw and vulnerable close-mic with whispered doubles, studio quality intimate atmospheric mix, 82 BPM, key of Em, reference Bon Iver rawness, reference Jonathan Ogden vulnerability, reference Jon Guerra honesty
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
Walked into the room and took a seat
Looked around the table, felt the heat
Everyone seemed certain, everyone belonged
Everyone but me — I must be wrong
Rehearsed my intro seven times last night
Still convinced they'd see right through the light

[Chorus]
Everyone at the table
Thinks everyone else belongs more
Everyone at the table
Is guarding the same locked door
Imposter, imposter
The feeling never goes away
You just learn to work alongside it
Day by day by day

[Verse 2]
Julie Zhuo — thirteen years at the book
IC designer to VP, every rung she took
Episode two of Lenny's show, she said
"I felt like a fraud inside my head"
Wrote The Making of a Manager from that pain
Turns out the doubt and growth share the same lane

[Chorus]
Everyone at the table
Thinks everyone else belongs more
Everyone at the table
Is guarding the same locked door
Imposter, imposter
The feeling never goes away
You just learn to work alongside it
Day by day by day

[Outro]
Your imposter syndrome is lying to you
But I know that doesn't make it quiet
Day by day by day
